PTNTL was Keem's fourth EP under the name Hykeem Carter but due to him deleting his projects from streaming platforms the EP has been entirely lost outside of one track. We also know nothing about the EP other then its cover/name and that it was released on June 27, 2017.
Midnight was Keem’s sixth EP under his birth name, but is only the 2nd thats been fully recovered. Just like his previous projects the Midnight EP has been deleted from all streaming platforms.
The cover to Keems 2017 EP No Name is the last to see a release under the name Hykeem Carter but still remains on Keem's SoundCloud unlike the others from this era.
After working on the Black Panther soundtrack and Jay Rock's "Redemption" Keem would work on and release the Hearts & Darts EP.

Following a name change from Hykeem Carter, to HiiFii, to Baby Keem, and the release of his eighth EP "Hearts and Darts" , Keem would shift his focus to the release of his first commercial mixtape, dubbed "The Sound of Bad Habit." CardoGotWings would reprise his role as a key player for the production, and produce all officially released songs except for "A New Day" and "Extra" along with Cardo Keem's cousin Kendrick Lamar would also be brought aboard to help with the recording process. The Sound of Bad Habit would be released on October 29, 2018.

"TOP 5" was first posted as a snippet to LEAKED.CX on August 11th, 2022, and hours later would be force-leaked during an attempted sale of the song. After being force-leaked, the file was tagged with nothing else but a cover; it's unknown if this cover was ever official.
“INVENTED IT” is the first of three singles for Baby Keems sophomore mixtape DIE FOR MY BITCH.

“FRANCE FREESTYLE” is the second single for his sophomore mixtape DIE FOR MY BITCH under the name of Baby Keem. In this song, we got Keem rapping with boastful lyrics over a raw deep 808 trap beat with piano parts who complement the heavy production in this track.
“ORANGE SODA” is Baby Keem’s breakout hit. Released as a single from DIE FOR MY BITCH in June 2019, the song quickly became a fan favorite and was certified platinum a year later.

Before Keem updated "DIE FOR MY BITCH", he released what fans call "Napster DFMB". It has some major differences from the DIE FOR MY BITCH on all streaming platforms with the biggest ones being a tracklist and cover art change, as well as three unreleased songs.

The mixtape was released on July 19th, 2019. Following the release of Sound of Bad Habit, Keem would begin work on his second commerical mixtape, DIE FOR MY BITCH. The album would largely be focused on destructive habits, toxic romance, and the never-ending pursuit of money and fame, with songs that are both aggressive and reflective. It would release on Napster first, with three tracks from the Napster version being replaced upon it's arrival to major streaming services.

hooligan / sons & critics freestyle is a two-track bundle serving as Baby Keem’s first release since soaring in popularity—and even being nominated as a 2020 XXL Freshman—thanks to his acclaimed July 2019 debut project DIE FOR MY BITCH. Only hooligan would see a release on The Melodic Blue and its Deluxe.

Donda is Kanye West’s tenth studio album, released on August 29, 2021. The record is named after Kanye’s late mother, Donda West. After a 15-month wait, the album finally ended up releasing almost two days after the final listening party, on the early morning of August 29, 2021, with the addition of four new remixes of songs on the album.

Name (Check out the Tracker website!):
The Melodic Blue [Cover]
(feat. Don Toliver, Kendrick Lamar, &Travis Scott)
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and: The Melodic Blue is Baby Keem’s debut studio album. The project has been in the works since late 2019 and was announced to be “on the way” via Instagram in early August 2020. The project would not be released for another 13 months. Following the release of the singles leading up to the album Keem began indicating that a release was imminent. Two days after featuring on Kanye Wests tenth studio album Donda Keem announced that he had turned the album in, and, on September 2, he stated that it would be arriving that month. The Melodic Blue was released on September 10, 2021, and would get a "DLC" on September 22, that added Brent Faiyaz to the track "lost souls". Two months after releasing his debut album, Keem earned himself four Grammy nominations.

Name (Check out the Tracker website!): Don Toliver - Life of a Don  [Cover]
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and: Life of a DON is Don Toliver’s second studio album and third official project, following the success of his 2020 debut album, Heaven Or Hell. The album name was officially announced on Don Toliver’s Instagram on September 28, 2021. Don Toliver released the first official single “What You Need” on May 4, 2021, which charted at #82 for a week on the Billboard Hot 100. On June 18, 2021, he dropped the second single “Drugs N Hella Melodies,” featuring Colombian-American singer Kali Uchis.

Notes (Join the Discord for updates and: Mr. Morale & The Big Steppers is the fifth studio album by American rapper Kendrick Lamar, released on May 13, 2022, by PGLang, Top Dawg Entertainment (TDE), Aftermath Entertainment, and Interscope Records. The double album is Lamar's first studio album release since DAMN. (2017), his first album release under his creative collective PGLang, and his final project with TDE (under which he released four albums and was one of their flagship artists). Mr Morale was released as a double album.
